deadAsDiscoBeginnerGuide.title: Dead as Disco Beginner Guide
deadAsDiscoBeginnerGuide.intro: Start your neon rhythm journey with these essential tips for combat, progression, and mastering the beat.

steps.title: Master the Rhythm
steps.description: Every action syncs to music - learn the beat for perfect dodges, attacks, and combos.

steps.title: Explore the Demo
steps.description: Try the free demo to understand core mechanics before buying the full game.

steps.title: Learn Basic Combos
steps.description: Master simple attack patterns to build up your score and rhythm efficiency.

steps.title: Unlock Early Skills
steps.description: Focus on essential abilities that improve movement and combat effectiveness.

quickTips: The demo has been played by over 1.4M players - join the rhythm revolution!
Music synchronization is key - attacks are stronger when perfectly timed.
Charlie Disco is your hero - master his unique abilities and fighting style.
Positive reviews show 93% of players love the rhythm combat system.

deadAsDiscoRhythmCombat.title: Dead as Disco Rhythm Combat
deadAsDiscoRhythmCombat.intro: Every move in Dead as Disco syncs to the beat. Master rhythm-based combat to maximize damage and combos.

cards.name: Music Sync
cards.description: All attacks, dodges, and combos are stronger when perfectly timed to the music.

cards.name: Combo System
cards.description: Chain attacks together to build up your multiplier and score higher points.

cards.name: Perfect Dodges
cards.description: Time your dodges perfectly to counter attacks and create openings for combos.

cards.name: Idol Bosses
cards.description: Each Idol has unique patterns and requires different rhythm strategies to defeat.

milestones: Master a full song without taking damage

deadAsDiscoIdols.title: Dead as Disco Idols
deadAsDiscoIdols.intro: Idols are powerful boss enemies with unique mechanics and rhythm patterns. Learn their patterns to conquer them.

bosses.name: Hemlock
bosses.description: The initial boss encounter that teaches basic rhythm combat mechanics.

bosses.name: Pattern Masters
bosses.description: Powerful Idols with complex attack patterns that require precise timing.

bosses.name: Music-Based Mechanics
bosses.description: Each Idol's attacks and abilities sync to different musical genres and tempos.

bosses.name: Progression Challenges
bosses.description: Idol fights become more difficult as you progress through the story mode.

skills.name: Combat Abilities

skills.name: Movement Skills

skills.name: Custom Builds

buildStrategies: Aggressive - Focus on high damage and fast combos
Balanced - Mix of offense and defense for versatility
Speed Run - Focus on mobility and quick eliminations

lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Dead as Disco Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Start with a clean first-hour plan: learn timing, clear Story Mode basics, then use Infinite Disco and My Music when your rhythm control is stable.

steps.title: Start with the demo or Early Access build
steps.description: Use the demo to feel Beat Kune Do timing quickly, then move to Early Access for full progression systems.

steps.title: Complete the tutorial before score chasing
steps.description: Treat the tutorial as mandatory calibration for attack cadence, defensive rhythm, and combo pacing.

steps.title: Use Story Mode as your learning path
steps.description: Story levels teach encounter flow and Idol pressure better than random challenge hopping.

steps.title: Prioritize survival over perfect rhythm
steps.description: If timing slips, keep movement clean and protect your health before forcing advanced sequences.

steps.title: Spend fan power on practical upgrades
steps.description: Pick survivability and reliable damage nodes first so each run feels steadier and less volatile.

steps.title: Check The Encore between milestones
steps.description: Return to the hub to manage unlocks, collect progress items, and follow narrative beats.

steps.title: Practice loops in Infinite Disco
steps.description: Use challenge runs to isolate weak habits and rebuild consistency without restarting campaign stages.

steps.title: Import custom songs after core mastery
steps.description: My Music works best once you can recognize timing drift and calibrate BPM settings confidently.

quickTips: Early Access released on May 5, 2026, so post-launch systems are already playable now.
Use short sessions focused on dodge timing to improve faster than long unfocused grinding.
The first Idol fights are better training tools than raw score farming in your first hours.
Treat official songs as calibration baselines before importing custom tracks.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Dead as Disco Combat Guide
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Beat Kune Do rewards controlled rhythm. Build fight consistency through readable patterns, defensive timing, and deliberate resource usage.

cards.name: Anchor your inputs to the beat
cards.description: Use a stable rhythm loop for attacks and dodges instead of mashing through visual noise.

cards.name: Recover calmly after timing misses
cards.description: One missed beat is recoverable. Re-center movement and restart with low-risk actions.

cards.name: Use perfect defense for momentum swings
cards.description: Clean dodge and counter timing creates space, resets pressure, and opens safer damage windows.

cards.name: Spend takedowns at pressure peaks
cards.description: Use finishers when crowds collapse on you or when elite enemies threaten your run stability.

cards.name: Route upgrades around your weak points
cards.description: If survival fails, take durability nodes first; if pace stalls, invest in combo extension tools.

cards.name: Use Idol abilities for crowd control
cards.description: Knockback and disruption tools matter most when enemy density makes perfect rhythm harder to maintain.

cards.name: Treat burst skills as finish windows
cards.description: Hold high-impact skills until enemy patterns are committed and punishment opportunities are clear.

cards.name: Read each Idol fight language first
cards.description: Every Idol encounter has distinct cadence and spacing rules, so first attempts should gather pattern data.

milestones: Land five clean defensive timings in one run
Clear a challenge without panic mashing
Convert one defensive opening into a full burst cycle
Finish an Idol phase with stable rhythm control

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Dead as Disco Controls and Moves
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Focus on move purpose before memorizing button labels. This list tracks what each action does and when it provides value in real combat.

items.name: Basic Attack Combo
items.description: Primary pressure chain for maintaining rhythm presence and damage uptime.

items.name: Dodge
items.description: Main survival tool for repositioning and setting up safe counterplay timing.

items.name: Perfect Counter
items.description: High-value defensive response that turns enemy commitment into your tempo advantage.

items.name: Takedown
items.description: Resource-based elimination move best saved for dangerous enemies or crowd resets.

items.name: Windmill Kick
items.description: Charged offensive option that adds burst pressure when openings are confirmed.

items.name: Drumstick Throw
items.description: Range check tool for tagging targets before committing to close-range sequences.

items.name: Drumstick Follow-Up
items.description: Instant re-entry option that converts ranged contact into direct melee pressure.

items.name: Fever Rush
items.description: Momentum skill for fast punish windows after enemy rhythm patterns are recognized.

items.name: Bass Invader
items.description: Knockback-style Idol power for lane control and defensive breathing room.

items.name: Selectable Dances
items.description: Customization-focused additions scheduled in roadmap updates for style variation.

items.name: On-Press Attack Option
items.description: Planned input behavior option intended to improve control comfort for more players.

items.name: Steam Deck and Controller Support
items.description: Controller and Deck-friendly setup with per-device binding checks in settings.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Dead as Disco Release Date, Patch Notes and Roadmap
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Track what is live now, what changed at launch, and which roadmap beats matter most for gameplay planning.

solutions.name: Early Access Launch
solutions.description: The Early Access build is live on PC storefronts, including core story and progression systems.

solutions.name: Launch Content Scope
solutions.description: Launch includes early Story Mode levels, launch Idols, The Encore, Infinite Disco, and My Music.

solutions.name: Launch Patch Notes
solutions.description: Initial notes emphasize progression tuning, challenge systems, optimization, and platform readiness.

solutions.name: Known Issues Window
solutions.description: Early hotfixes target custom song behavior, arena display issues, and challenge-state edge cases.

solutions.name: Update 1
solutions.description: Planned focus on Infinite Disco and My Music expansion, including playlist and arena improvements.

solutions.name: Update 2
solutions.description: Planned focus on customization depth, collectible additions, and balance-oriented ability tuning.

solutions.name: Update 3
solutions.description: Planned XL patch adding a new Idol arc, music content, fresh enemies, and progression additions.

solutions.name: Road to 1.0
solutions.description: Future festivals and major updates are planned to converge into the full launch narrative arc.

managementTips: If a system behaves unexpectedly after launch, check the latest official patch note before reworking your setup.
Treat roadmap dates as planning anchors for build resets and new challenge practice cycles.
Prioritize currently playable loops instead of waiting for future content drops.
Use launch patch changes to update your upgrade path and preferred challenge routing.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Dead as Disco Boss Idols Guide
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Dead as Disco starts Charlie Disco's comeback with four launch Idols and more planned during Early Access. Use this guide to prepare each encounter, understand fight identity, and plan ability unlock priorities.

cards.name: Hemlock
cards.description: Available in Early Access. Former punk rocker and Harmony Corp enforcer. His subway-concert route emphasizes fan-wave control and unlocks the knockback-focused Bass Invader ability.

cards.name: Arora
cards.description: Available in Early Access. Next-gen AI pop idol with clone pressure and fast phase shifts. Defeating Arora opens an Idol ability route for expanded special-move options.

cards.name: Dex
cards.description: Available in Early Access. Cybernetically enhanced guitar icon with aggressive tempo control. Clearing this route unlocks another dedicated Idol skill tree for build variety.

cards.name: Prophet
cards.description: Available in Early Access. Rapper-producer kingmaker focused on fan gauntlets and combo discipline. Prophet's defensive block path is valuable for parry timing and counter windows.

cards.name: Future Boss Idols
cards.description: Planned during Early Access. Additional former bandmates are expected to arrive with festival-style updates, new narrative arcs, fresh enemies, new skills, and signature abilities.

highlights: 4 of 7 Idols are live at Early Access launch
Each Idol route adds a distinct ability path
Summer Idol finale update is planned
More narrative arcs are planned before 1.0

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Dead as Disco Best Skills and Abilities
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Prioritize survivability first, then scale into pressure tools and specialized Idol paths. This progression keeps early clears stable while preparing for harder challenge pacing.

regions.name: Core First: More Hearts, Fever Capacity, Attack Boosts
regions.description: Best for beginners learning dodge timing and on-beat attacks. These picks create margin for mistakes and improve special-move uptime in long fights.

regions.name: High-Impact Moves: Charged Windmill Kick, Drumstick Toss Follow-Up
regions.description: Use these to open burst windows, close distance quickly, and convert rhythm openings into cleaner combo routes.

regions.name: Boss Ability Focus: Bass Invader, Prophet Block
regions.description: Most valuable when arenas get crowded or bosses force strict defensive timing. They help create space and generate reliable counterattack windows.

regions.name: Evolving Meta: Arora Tree, Dex Tree, Future Idol Abilities
regions.description: Revisit these as updates land. New Idol drops and ability passes can shift the best path for aggressive, utility, or score-focused playstyles.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Dead as Disco Story Levels Walkthrough
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Follow this chapter path to learn rhythm fundamentals early, clear each launch Idol route, and prepare for future story expansion during Early Access and the planned 1.0 arc.

creatures.name: Tutorial
creatures.description: Learn Beat Kune Do fundamentals: attack cadence, dodge timing, counter flow, Fever building, and on-beat consistency.

creatures.name: Hemlock Story Level
creatures.description: Underground concert route with train setpieces and crowd pressure. Ideal for learning wave control and boss-timing fundamentals.

creatures.name: Arora Story Level
creatures.description: AI pop spectacle focused on clone pressure, visual shifts, and multi-phase rhythm windows that test lane awareness and recovery discipline.

creatures.name: Dex Story Level
creatures.description: Guitar-driven encounter route with aggressive arena pacing and heavier boss pressure. Rewards calm defense and deliberate punish timing.

creatures.name: Prophet Story Level
creatures.description: Rap-producer showdown with dense fan gauntlets and combo control checks, while expanding the band-history narrative context.

creatures.name: The Encore Hub
creatures.description: Restore the dive bar, spend fan energy, unlock cosmetics, and collect memorabilia while tracking core story clues between combat routes.

creatures.name: Future Idol Story Updates
creatures.description: Festival-style additions are planned to introduce new Idol arcs, full music-video levels, extra enemies, collectibles, skills, and narrative beats.

creatures.name: 1.0 Final Narrative Arc
creatures.description: Planned for 2027 as the full narrative climax, including final secrets, refreshed progression flow, and the endgame confrontation with Harmony Corp.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Dead as Disco Infinite Disco Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Infinite Disco is the replay sandbox for official tracks, custom music experiments, leaderboard pushes, and challenge practice loops. Use it to sharpen execution outside campaign progression.

items.name: Official Song Runs
items.description: Replay official Dead as Disco tracks outside Story Mode. Early Access promotes 30+ songs across originals, covers, and licensed tracks.

items.name: Song Challenges
items.description: Targeted objectives for practicing special moves, ability routing, and song-specific consistency while earning fan energy.

items.name: My Music
items.description: Import personal tracks and build music-synced gameplay around them for personalized replay loops.

items.name: Manual BPM Calibration
items.description: Calibrate imported songs before combat. Auto BPM improvements are planned, but manual BPM remains the practical setup path today.

items.name: Friend and Global Leaderboards
items.description: Compare runs across songs and challenges. Seasonal leaderboard resets are planned as balance updates evolve.

items.name: Arena Mods and Arena Swaps
items.description: Adjust arena feel after mastering baseline song loops. Roadmap updates mention additional arenas, challenge mods, and improved arena-swap flow.

items.name: Custom Challenge Playlists
items.description: Planned update feature for chaining multiple My Music challenges into replayable setlists.

items.name: Free Play Practice
items.description: Use this mode to groove, test builds, and stabilize crowd-control timing before high-pressure Idol or leaderboard attempts.

unlockMilestones: Clear your first Infinite Disco challenge route
Upload a score to global leaderboards
Complete one stable custom-song calibration session
Finish a full practice loop with consistent defensive timing

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Dead as Disco My Music Custom Songs
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Use My Music to bring local tracks into Infinite Disco, then tune BPM and beat offset so attacks land on rhythm.

sections.name: Step 1: Prepare a local MP3
sections.description: Choose a clean file with a steady pulse. Songs with long silence, heavy tempo shifts, or messy intros are harder to sync.

sections.name: Step 2: Open Infinite Disco and import
sections.description: Go to Infinite Disco, switch to Free Play, and use Add My Music or Import Song to load your local track.

sections.name: Step 3: Save the track before deep edits
sections.description: Save the imported song into your list first so testing and retries stay quick and organized.

sections.name: Step 4: Set BPM before anything else
sections.description: Enter the known BPM or tap it manually in the song editor. Start with whole-number BPM values for the cleanest baseline.

sections.name: Step 5: Adjust beat offset
sections.description: If timing feels close but early or late, adjust Beat Offset after BPM is set. Offset is mainly for delayed intros and start silence.

sections.name: Step 6: Fine tune with the advanced editor
sections.description: Use waveform view, metronome, and short replay loops. If beat lines drift over time, recheck BPM or switch to a steadier song.

growthMilestones: Song feels too fast or too slow: recheck BPM, then test half or double BPM if needed.
Beat feels slightly early or late: keep BPM and adjust Beat Offset in small steps.
Timing breaks after intro silence: trim the start or offset to the first clear beat.
Sync drifts over time: BPM is likely wrong or the song has tempo variation.
Import fails or sounds broken: re-export as a standard MP3 and import again.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Dead as Disco Soundtrack and Streamer Safe Mode
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Dead as Disco includes original tracks, licensed songs, covers, and creator-focused safety options. Use this comparison to pick the right listening setup.

priorities.name: In-game music library
priorities.description: Includes original songs, streamer-safe originals, covers, and licensed tracks used in rhythm combat.

priorities.name: Streamer Safe Mode
priorities.description: Enable this in Settings > Audio before livestreams or VOD recording when you want to avoid licensed-track issues.

priorities.name: Streamer Safe Soundtrack Vol. 1 DLC
priorities.description: Contains 15 original songs, 5 bonus tracks, and wallpapers. This package excludes cover songs and licensed tracks.

priorities.name: Streamer Safe Soundtrack pricing
priorities.description: Standard price is $9.99, with a launch discount to $7.99 during the initial release window.

priorities.name: One Night Only Radio Play Vol. 1
priorities.description: A 27-track release for Spotify, YouTube Music, Apple Music, and iTunes for listening outside the game.

priorities.name: Licensed music boundaries
priorities.description: Licensed song rights are separate from streamer-safe content, so use Streamer Safe mode instead of assuming all tracks are broadcast-safe.

priorities.name: My Music imported tracks
priorities.description: Local files you import are separate from official soundtrack releases and depend on your own usage rights.

priorities.name: Future music updates
priorities.description: Roadmap updates include more licensed tracks, more covers, and additional streamer-safe originals.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Dead as Disco Steam Deck Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Dead as Disco is listed as Steam Deck supported. Use this setup path for smooth play and reliable custom-song imports.

achievements.title: Step 1: Install and update the game
achievements.description: Install from Steam and finish the latest patch before performance testing or custom-song setup.

achievements.title: Step 2: Start with default Deck controls
achievements.description: Run tutorial or Infinite Disco first, then adjust only if timing and dodge rhythm feel inconsistent.

achievements.title: Step 3: Use Desktop Mode for file work
achievements.description: Switch to Desktop Mode to move MP3 files. It is much easier than browsing deep folders from in-game menus.

groups.name: Custom Song Path and Quick Checks

achievements.title: Step 5: Verify compatdata folder
achievements.description: If 3404260 is missing, inspect /home/deck/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/ and check the newest app folders after launch.

achievements.title: Step 6: Return to Gaming Mode and test
achievements.description: Launch the game, load My Music in Infinite Disco, then set BPM first and Beat Offset second for stable timing.

achievements.title: Quick check: Song missing
achievements.description: Enable hidden folders, recheck the exact path, and confirm the file is inside MusicFiles.

achievements.title: Quick check: Song drifts or feels off-beat
achievements.description: Re-enter BPM, then adjust Beat Offset in small increments. Re-export problem files as standard MP3.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Dead as Disco Demo, Price and Editions
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Compare the free demo, Early Access pricing, soundtrack DLC, and bundle options before you buy.

faqs.question: Does Dead as Disco have a free demo?
faqs.answer: Yes. The demo is free and includes tutorial content, a sample story mission, Infinite Disco, and custom-song support in Free Play.

faqs.question: What is the base game price in Early Access?
faqs.answer: The standard base game price is $24.99, with a launch offer at $19.99 that was listed through May 19, 2026.

faqs.question: How much is the Streamer Safe Soundtrack Vol. 1 DLC?
faqs.answer: The DLC is listed at $9.99 standard, with a launch discount shown at $7.99.

faqs.question: What is in the Dead as Disco Superfan Bundle?
faqs.answer: It combines the base game and Streamer Safe Soundtrack Vol. 1, with a launch-period bundled value around $25.18.

faqs.question: Should I buy now or wait for version 1.0?
faqs.answer: Use the demo first if you are unsure, buy Early Access if you want current content and upcoming additions, or wait for 1.0 if you prefer a fuller campaign release.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Dead as Disco Co-op Multiplayer Status
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Current play is centered on solo progression and challenge loops, with community attention on future multiplayer direction.

faqs.question: Is the current build single-player?
faqs.answer: The present gameplay experience is designed around solo rhythm-combat progression.

faqs.question: Can you play co-op right now?
faqs.answer: Co-op is not currently a live gameplay mode in the Early Access build.

faqs.question: Is multiplayer being discussed for future updates?
faqs.answer: Future roadmap conversations include ongoing feature expansion, and players continue to watch for multiplayer announcements.

faqs.question: What should group-focused players do for now?
faqs.answer: Track roadmap updates while using challenge leaderboards and community events as shared progression goals.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Dead as Disco

questions.question: Is Dead as Disco a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Dead as Disco is currently a singleplayer experience focused on rhythm combat and boss battles.

questions.question: What platforms is Dead as Disco available on?
questions.answer: Dead as Disco is available on PC via Steam Early Access. It may come to other platforms in the future.

questions.question: How does the rhythm combat system work?
questions.answer: All attacks, dodges, and combos sync to the music. Perfect timing does more damage and builds higher scores.

questions.question: How much does Dead as Disco cost?
questions.answer: The game is priced at its standard Steam price. Check the Steam store for current pricing.

questions.question: Does Dead as Disco have controller support?
questions.answer: Yes, the game supports controllers, though keyboard and mouse may offer more precise timing for rhythm combat.

questions.question: Will Dead as Disco receive updates after launch?
questions.answer: Yes, the developers plan to continue updating the game with new Idols, songs, and features post-launch.
